channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Bank of England Holds Rates but Warns of Potential Future Hikes Due to Inflation Risks
item.description: The Bank of England has kept its interest rates steady at the current level but issued a significant warning about possible future rate hikes amid heightened economic uncertainties. According to the c...

item.title: New Commander Takes Over the Army's 4th Infantry Division in Region 10
item.description: The Army’s 4th Infantry Division has officially welcomed Brig. Gen. Siegfred Tubalado as its acting commander, following a turnover ceremony on April 29, 2026, at Camp Evangelista in Cagayan de Oro Ci...

item.title: Three family members die following suspected domestic violence incident in Rosemeadow
item.description: In a tragic incident in Rosemeadow, a suburb in Sydney's south-west, three members of the same family have died following an alleged domestic violence attack. Police were called to the property in the...

item.title: Trump reviews Iranian peace proposal as ceasefire tensions and risk of renewed conflict persist
item.description: The United States and Iran remain in a fragile standoff despite a temporary ceasefire, with President Donald Trump signalling scepticism over a new peace proposal from Tehran. Trump stated that he wou...

item.title: TikToker Kevin Kinuthia Buys Scooter and Shares His Excitement with Fans
item.description: Kenyan TikToker Kevin Kinuthia recently shared his excitement after purchasing a new electric scooter, showing off its number plate in a video posted online. At 24 years old, Kinuthia is no stranger t...

item.title: Media controversies, Woolworths court documents, and US passport design spark debate
item.description: This Crikey “Tips and Murmurs” column pulls together several loosely connected media and business stories, each highlighting questionable decisions or eyebrow-raising moments. One focus is a court cas...

item.title: Families of the missing in Manipur continue to wait three years after ethnic violence
item.description: It has been three years since the ethnic violence erupted in Manipur, but many families of missing persons are still struggling with uncertainty and grief. The conflict displaced numerous people, leav...

item.title: Anti-corruption group holds Makati march urging accountability and impeachment of corrupt officials
item.description: Nagtipon ang isang anti-corruption group sa Makati City upang ipanawagan ang mas matibay na pananagutan ng pamahalaan at ang pagpapatalsik o impeachment ng mga opisyal na sangkot umano sa katiwalian. ...

item.title: Tips for Affordable Access to Airport Lounges
item.description: Many travelers dream of enjoying the comfort of airport lounges, but access is often expensive and restricted to first-class passengers or loyalty program members. This guide explores practical strate...

item.title: Coatue Launches Venture to Acquire Land for Data Centers in Collaboration with Cloud Startups
item.description: Coatue, a major venture capital and hedge fund firm, is expanding its strategy in the AI sector beyond its investments in companies like Anthropic, OpenAI, xAI, and data center operators such as DayOn...

item.title: Protecting Workers During Economic and Global Challenges
item.description: This editorial stresses the importance of safeguarding workers’ rights in South Africa, emphasising that protecting those who sell their labour is fundamental to a civilised society. Despite the count...

item.title: Helmsman explains circumstances leading to Jabalpur cruise boat capsize
item.description: The tragic capsize of the Narmada Queen at Bargi Dam in Jabalpur, Madhya Pradesh, has claimed 11 lives, including children, as rescue divers recovered more bodies. Helmsman Mahesh Patel, with 20 years...

item.title: Ukrainian Soldier Survives Two Weeks Hidden in Russian Dugout
item.description: Vadym Lietunov, a 34-year-old corporal from Odesa, survived an extraordinary two weeks in a Russian dugout after his position was destroyed by enemy drones and mortars. Following a deadly drone strike...

item.title: Austrian Authorities Arrest Man Suspected of Poisoning Baby Food Jars
item.description: Austria don arrest one man wey dem dey suspect say him put rat poison for baby food jars as part of extortion plan. The man, wey be 39 years old, dem catch am for Burgenland, south of Vienna. Police n...

item.title: Pantami suspends social media aide over unauthorized post calling opponents hypocrites
item.description: Isa Pantami, wey be former Minister of Communication and Digital Economy, don suspend one of him social media aides indefinitely. This na because the aide post for Pantami verified Facebook page wey d...

item.title: US plans troop reduction in Germany spark concern among European allies and US lawmakers
item.description: Germany Defence Minister Boris Pistorius has said the planned reduction of 5,000 US troops from Germany should motivate Europe to take stronger responsibility for its own security. Currently, the US h...

item.title: Highlighting Filipino Artists Performing at Coachella and Lollapalooza
item.description: ABS-CBN’s One Music PH 360 features a special segment focusing on Filipino artists who have made their mark internationally by performing at major music festivals like Coachella and Lollapalooza. This...

item.title: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio to meet Vatican officials after Trump-Pope tensions
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio is scheduled to visit Rome and the Vatican this week, according to an Italian government source. The visit comes shortly after former US President Donald Trump public...

item.title: AAP’s Contributions to Education and Health Strengthen Indian Democracy
item.description: The defection of seven Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) members to the Bharatiya Janata Party has led to criticism of the defectors, the party leadership, and AAP's stance on majoritarian politics. While these c...

item.title: Challenges and Risks of Using Russia's Northern Sea Route for International Shipping
item.description: Russia is promoting its Northern Sea Route (NSR) as a shorter alternative between Europe and Asia, potentially cutting travel distance by up to 40% compared to the Suez Canal. Despite the promise of f...
